heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 566 Days: AIFA Train Nationalized as 'Tren Felipe Ángeles'; Luisa Alcalde Named Legal Counsel; Formal Note Sent to U.S. Over CIA Chihuahua Operation
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum's April 23 mañanera announced three major developments: the federal government bought out Spain's CAF and Omnitren from the Suburban Train for 5,999 million pesos, with the Buenavista–AIFA express renamed 'Tren Felipe Ángeles' and set to inaugurate April 26 ahead of the FIFA World Cup. Separately, she confirmed Luisa María Alcalde — former SEGOB secretary and Morena national president — will take over as Legal Counsel to the Presidency from May 1, replacing Esthela Damián who left for a Guerrero candidacy. On the sovereignty front, Mexico delivered a formal diplomatic note to U.S. Ambassador Ken Salazar over the unauthorized CIA operation in Chihuahua that killed two CIA agents and two Mexican AEI investigators on April 20, with Sheinbaum weighing sanctions against the Chihuahua state government for bypassing federal authorization. The peso eased slightly to ~17.35 MXN/USD amid the bilateral tension.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, INAH, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder, PBS NewsHour, NBC News, Time, NPR, CBC, Japan Kantei, Japan MOFA, SDP Noticias, Eje Central, TVAzteca, UnoTV, CBS News, El Mañana de Nuevo Laredo
